text: The patient is positioned supine on the operating table with both arms tucked. The robotic system is docked over the head, with the camera port placed midline above the pubic bone. Two robotic arms are docked in the left and right lower quadrants of the abdomen.

Following abdominal insufflation, the surgeon incises the peritoneum circumferentially around the hernia defect, entering the preperitoneal plane. Using a monopolar cautery hook, the surgeon dissects the preperitoneal space laterally beyond the rectus abdominis muscle until reaching the linea semilunaris, with clear visualization maintained throughout the procedure.

The surgeon mobilizes the right rectus muscle and excises the hernia sac at the midline. Attention is then shifted to the left side of the hernia, where the preperitoneal plane is entered. Instruments are exchanged between the robotic arms to facilitate left lateral dissection, extending to the linea semilunaris, with exceptional visualization of the surgical field.

The surgeon completes bilateral retro-rectus muscle release and re-approximates the midline fascia without tension using a unidirectional barbed suture. A biosynthetic mesh is placed in a sublay position for reinforcement and fixated to the rectus sheath with two interrupted sutures. The posterior sheath is closed with the same suture in a running fashion.
